content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 28 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 224–227 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_028_page_001.jpg, DEU_028_page_002.jpg, DEU_028_page_003.jpg, DEU_028_page_004.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 159–161 — DEU_028_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_028_vaticanus_page_002.jpg, DEU_028_vaticanus_page_003.jpg

---

**1** And it shall be, if you diligently listen to the voice of the LORD your God, to keep [and] to do all his commandments that I am commanding you today, the LORD your God will set you high above all the nations of the earth.

**2** And all these blessings shall come upon you and overtake you, if you listen to the voice of the LORD your God:

**3** Blessed shall you be in the city, and blessed shall you be in the field.

**4** Blessed shall be the fruit of your womb, and the fruit of your ground, and the fruit of your livestock — the offspring of your cattle and the young of your flock.

**5** Blessed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.

**6** Blessed shall you be when you come in, and blessed shall you be when you go out.

**7** The LORD will cause the enemies who rise up against you to be struck before your face; by one road they shall come out against you, and by seven roads they shall flee before you.

**8** The LORD will command the blessing to be upon you in your storehouses and in all to which you set your hand; and he will bless you in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

**9** The LORD will establish you as a holy people for himself, as he has sworn to you, if you keep the commandments of the LORD your God and walk in his ways.

**10** And all the peoples of the earth shall see that the name of the LORD is called over you, and they shall be in awe of you.

**11** And the LORD will make you abound in good things — in the fruit of your womb and in the fruit of your livestock and in the fruit of your ground — upon the land that the LORD swore to your fathers to give you.

**12** The LORD will open to you his good treasure, the heavens, to give the rain of your land in its season and to bless all the work of your hand; and you shall lend to many nations, but you shall not borrow.

**13** And the LORD will make you the head and not the tail, and you shall be only above and not below, if you listen to the commandments of the LORD your God that I am commanding you today, to keep [them] and to do [them],

**14** and you do not turn aside from any of the words that I am commanding you today, to the right or to the left, to walk after other gods to serve them.

**15** But it shall be, if you do not listen to the voice of the LORD your God, to keep [and] to do all his commandments and his statutes that I am commanding you today, all these curses shall come upon you and overtake you:

**16** Cursed shall you be in the city, and cursed shall you be in the field.

**17** Cursed shall be your basket and your kneading bowl.

**18** Cursed shall be the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, the offspring of your cattle and the young of your flock.

**19** Cursed shall you be when you come in, and cursed shall you be when you go out.

**20** The LORD will send upon you the curse [me'erah], the panic, and the rebuke in all to which you set your hand and which you do, until you are destroyed and until you perish quickly, because of the evil of your deeds by which you have forsaken me.

**21** The LORD will make the plague cling to you until he has consumed you from upon the land that you are entering to possess.

**22** The LORD will strike you with wasting disease and with fever and with inflammation and with burning heat and with sword [drought] and with blight and with mildew [yerakon]; and they shall pursue you until you perish.

**23** And your heavens that are over your head shall be bronze, and the earth beneath you shall be iron.

**24** The LORD will make the rain of your land powder and dust; from the heavens it shall come down upon you until you are destroyed.

**25** The LORD will cause you to be struck before your enemies; by one road you shall go out against him, and by seven roads you shall flee before him; and you shall be a thing of horror [za'avah] to all the kingdoms of the earth.

**26** And your carcass shall be food for every bird of the heavens and for the beast of the earth, and there shall be no one to frighten [them] away.

**27** The LORD will strike you with the boils of Mitsrayim, and with tumors, and with scurvy [garav], and with itch [cheres], from which you cannot be healed.

**28** The LORD will strike you with madness and with blindness and with confusion of heart.

**29** And you shall grope at midday as the blind man gropes in darkness, and you shall not prosper in your ways; and you shall be only oppressed and plundered all the days, and there shall be no one to save [you].

**30** You shall betroth a wife, but another man shall lie with her; you shall build a house, but you shall not dwell in it; you shall plant a vineyard, but you shall not enjoy [use/profane] it.

**31** Your ox shall be slaughtered before your eyes, but you shall not eat of it; your donkey shall be seized before your face and shall not return to you; your flock shall be given to your enemies, and there shall be no one to save you.

**32** Your sons and your daughters shall be given to another people, and your eyes shall look and fail for them all the day; and there shall be no strength in your hand.

**33** The fruit of your ground and all your toil shall a nation that you have not known eat; and you shall be only oppressed and crushed all the days.

**34** And you shall be driven mad by the sight of your eyes that you see.

**35** The LORD will strike you with a severe boil on the knees and on the legs, from which you cannot be healed — from the sole of your foot to the top of your head.

**36** The LORD will bring you and your king whom you set over yourself to a nation that neither you nor your fathers have known; and there you shall serve other gods — wood and stone.

**37** And you shall become a horror, a proverb, and a taunt among all the peoples to whom the LORD will lead you away.

**38** You shall carry much seed out to the field, but you shall gather in little, for the locust shall consume it.

**39** You shall plant vineyards and work [them], but you shall neither drink the wine nor gather [the grapes], for the worm shall eat them.

**40** You shall have olive trees throughout all your territory, but you shall not anoint yourself with oil, for your olives shall drop off.

**41** You shall father sons and daughters, but they shall not be yours, for they shall go into captivity.

**42** All your trees and the fruit of your ground the cicada [tselatsal] shall possess.

**43** The sojourner who is in your midst shall rise higher and higher above you, and you shall go down lower and lower.

**44** He shall lend to you, but you shall not lend to him; he shall be the head, and you shall be the tail.

**45** And all these curses shall come upon you and pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed, because you did not listen to the voice of the LORD your God, to keep his commandments and his statutes that he commanded you.

**46** And they shall be upon you as a sign and as a wonder, and upon your offspring forever.

**47** Because you did not serve the LORD your God with joyfulness and with gladness of heart, because of the abundance of all things —

**48** therefore you shall serve your enemies, whom the LORD will send against you, in hunger and in thirst and in nakedness and in want of everything; and he will put an iron yoke upon your neck until he has destroyed you.

**49** The LORD will raise against you a nation from afar, from the end of the earth, as the eagle swoops down [darting] — a nation whose tongue you shall not understand —

**50** a nation fierce of countenance, who does not respect the elder and does not show favor to the young.

**51** And he shall eat the offspring of your livestock and the fruit of your ground until you are destroyed; he shall not leave you grain, new wine, or oil, the offspring of your cattle or the young of your flock, until he has caused you to perish.

**52** And he shall besiege you within all your gates until your high and fortified walls fall down, in which you trusted, throughout all your land; and he shall besiege you within all your gates in all your land that the LORD your God has given you.

**53** And you shall eat the fruit of your womb — the flesh of your sons and your daughters, whom the LORD your God has given you — in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy shall distress you.

**54** The man among you who is most tender and most highly refined shall look grudgingly upon his brother and upon the wife of his bosom and upon the rest of his children whom he still has left —

**55** from giving to any of them of the flesh of his children that he is eating, because there is nothing left to him in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy shall distress you within all your gates.

**56** The most tender and refined woman among you — who would not venture to set the sole of her foot upon the ground because of refinement and tenderness — shall look grudgingly upon the husband of her bosom and upon her son and upon her daughter,

**57** and upon her afterbirth that goes out from between her feet, and upon her children whom she bears, for she shall eat them in secret for lack of everything, in the siege and in the distress with which your enemy shall distress you within your gates.

**58** If you are not careful to do all the words of this Torah that are written in this book — to fear this glorious and awesome name: the LORD your God —

**59** then the LORD will make your plagues extraordinary and the plagues of your offspring extraordinary — great and lasting plagues, and evil and lasting sicknesses.

**60** And he will bring back upon you all the diseases of Mitsrayim, of which you were afraid, and they shall cling to you.

**61** Also every sickness and every plague that is not written in the scroll of this Torah, the LORD will bring upon you, until you are destroyed.

**62** And you shall be left few in number, whereas you were like the stars of the heavens in multitude — because you did not listen to the voice of the LORD your God.

**63** And it shall be, just as the LORD rejoiced over you to do you good and to multiply you, so the LORD will rejoice over you to cause you to perish and to destroy you; and you shall be plucked from off the land that you are entering to possess.

**64** And the LORD will scatter you among all the peoples, from one end of the earth to the other end of the earth; and there you shall serve other gods that you have not known — you nor your fathers — wood and stone.

**65** And among those nations you shall find no rest, and there shall be no resting place for the sole of your foot; and the LORD will give you there a trembling heart and failing eyes and a languishing soul [nephesh].

**66** And your life shall hang in doubt before you; and you shall be in dread night and day, and you shall have no assurance of your life.

**67** In the morning you shall say: Would that it were evening! And in the evening you shall say: Would that it were morning! — because of the dread of your heart that you dread and the sight of your eyes that you see.

**68** And the LORD will bring you back to Mitsrayim in ships, by the road of which I said to you: You shall not see it again; and there you shall offer yourselves for sale to your enemies as male slaves and as female slaves, but there shall be no buyer.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**Structure — Blessings (vv.1–14) vs. Curses (vv.15–68):** The tochechah (rebuke/covenant curses) is the longest chapter in Deuteronomy (68 verses). The blessings occupy 14 verses; the curses 54. EMET notes the deliberate asymmetry: blessing is brief, curse is extensive. A similar tochechah appears in Lev 26. This is the ha-tochechah ha-gedolah (the Great Rebuke).

**V.1 — "Diligently listen" (shamoa tishma):** The Hebrew infinitive absolute shamo'a reinforces tishma — "if you really listen." EMET renders literally "diligently listen." LXX: εαν δε ακοη εισακουσης — exact same construction.

**V.22 — "Sword" (cherev) vs. drought:** Some MSS and commentators read cherev here as "drought/dryness" (charavah), not "sword." The context is a list of natural afflictions, suggesting the agricultural sense. EMET retains the consonantal text cherev with a bracket indicating ambiguity: [sword/drought].

**V.23 — Bronze sky, iron earth:** A powerful reversal of agricultural productivity — the sky that should give rain becomes hard metal; the earth that should be fruitful becomes unyielding metal. LXX: ο ουρανος σου χαλκους, η γη σου σιδηρα — precise.

**V.36 — The king:** This verse presupposes the monarchy (v.36 "your king whom you set over yourself") — consistent with Deut 17's anticipation of kingship. Historical fulfillment commonly read in the Assyrian deportation of Israel (722 BCE) and Babylonian exile of Judah (586 BCE).

**V.49 — "As the eagle swoops":** Hebrew ka'asher yid'eh ha-nesher — the eagle/vulture image of a conquering nation striking from afar. LXX: ον τροπον ορμηση αετος — "as an eagle swoops."

**V.53–57 — Siege cannibalism:** The text describes consumption of one's own children during siege. This is graphic covenant-curse language; similar to Lev 26:29, and also reflects historical record of siege conditions (2 Kings 6:28–29). LXX Vaticanus presents the full text without softening.

**V.58 — "This glorious and awesome name":** Hebrew et ha-shem ha-nichbad ve-ha-nora ha-zeh — "this honored and fearful name." The Name of the LORD as itself the object of fear. LXX: το ονομα το εντιμον και το θαυμαστον τουτο — "this honored and wonderful name."

**V.68 — Return to Mitsrayim in ships:** An unusual image — the return by sea contrasts with the exodus on foot through the wilderness. LXX adds specificity: εν πλοιοις — "in ships." The verse may refer to Jewish deportations to Egypt after 586 BCE, or to the slave trade.

— EMET
